---
title: "Models"
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: false
execute:
  echo: true
  eval: true
---

# Models {.center}

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
## Go to the lab

- **[Interactive lab notebook](./01_models.ipynb)** — run all code here
- **Exercise:** [Ex1: Models](./exercises/ex1_models.qmd)
- **Colab (external repo):** [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/HassanAlgoz/B5/blob/main/content/W4/M1/01_models.ipynb)
:::

---

## Environment setup

### Install dependencies

**A. On Colab?**

```python
%pip install -q langchain langchain_openai langchain_community
```

**B. Locally?**

If using `uv` locally you can `uv sync` (provided you have the `pyproject.toml`).

---

## Environment variables

| Variable             | Required | Purpose                                    |
|----------------------|----------|--------------------------------------------|
| `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Yes      | Authenticates requests to OpenRouter LLMs |

**On Colab?** Store as Colab **Secrets** (key icon), names as above. Grant this notebook access.

**Locally?** Use a `.env` file (in `.gitignore`); load with `python-dotenv`.

::: {.callout-warning}
API keys are secrets and shall [**never be exposed**](./never_expose_api_keys.qmd).
:::

---

## Reading environment variables

```python
import os
```

```python
try:
    # In Colab? read from userdata (secrets)
    from google.colab import userdata
    ON_COLAB = True
    os.environ["OPENROUTER_API_KEY"] = userdata.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")
except ImportError:
    # Load `.env` file (locally)
    from dotenv import load_dotenv
    load_dotenv(override=True)
```

---

## OpenRouter

[OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/) is the unified interface for LLMs.

- One API for all models — change a string to switch models
- Some models are free
- Can help with regional restrictions

Keys: [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)

![OpenRouter — unified API](assets/open_router.png){.r-stretch fig-alt="Diagram of OpenRouter as a gateway to multiple LLM providers"}

---

## Basic usage

1. **Standalone** — call models directly for generation, classification, extraction, etc.
2. **With agents** — specify the model when creating an agent (*later in the course*)

LangChain integrates many providers; this course uses [ChatOpenAI](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/chat/openai) pointed at OpenRouter.

```python
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
```

```python
# https://openrouter.ai/openai/gpt-5-nano
model_gpt5_nano = ChatOpenAI(
    model="openai/gpt-5-nano",
    temperature=0,
    base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"),
)
```

---

## Choosing between models

**Quality**, **latency**, **price**, and **performance** vary.

- [Arena.ai](https://arena.ai/) — blind human comparisons
- [OpenRouter models](https://openrouter.ai/models) — pricing and trends
- [Model recommendation | Artificial Analysis](https://artificialanalysis.ai/models/recommend)

### Arabic-focused leaderboards

- [OALL | Open Arabic LLM Leaderboard](https://huggingface.co/OALL)
- [QIMMA leaderboard](https://huggingface.co/spaces/qimma/leaderboard)
- [Arabic LLM Leaderboard | Silma](https://silma.ai/arabic-llm-leaderboard)
- [Balsam benchmarks](https://benchmarks.ksaa.gov.sa/b/balsam?version=2)

---

## Model parameters


::: {.columns}

::: {.column}

Common [LangChain chat model parameters](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models#parameters):

- `model` — model id
- `max_tokens` — cap on response length
- `temperature` — sampling randomness
  - **Low (~0)** — deterministic, good for accuracy
  - **High (~1)** — more variety, good for creativity


:::

::: {.column}

![Low vs high temperature sampling](assets/temperature_1.png){fig-alt="Visualization of probability distributions for low vs high temperature"}

:::

:::

---

## Example: free model on OpenRouter

Filtering for **free** models: [NVIDIA Nemotron-3 Nano](https://openrouter.ai/nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b:free)

```python
model_nemotron3_nano = ChatOpenAI(
    model="nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-30b-a3b:free",
    temperature=0,
    base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1",
    api_key=os.environ.get("OPENROUTER_API_KEY"),
)
```

---

## Aside: running a model locally

::: {.callout-note}
LangChain can run models locally — privacy, custom weights, or cost.

[Ollama](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/integrations/chat/ollama) is a simple path for chat models on your machine.

On typical laptops (16–32 GB RAM), starting points for **tool-calling** models:

- `lfm2.5-thinking:1.2b`
- `granite4:3b` (or `granite4:1b` / `granite4:350m`)
- `ministral-3:3b`

Browse [ollama.com/models](https://ollama.com/models) with the **Tools** tag and small sizes (≤3B).
:::

---

## Models' key methods

Chat models subclass [Runnable](https://reference.langchain.com/python/langchain-core/runnables/base/Runnable):

1. [`.invoke()`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models#invocation) — full response when generation finishes
2. [`.stream()`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models#stream) — tokens/chunks as they arrive
3. [`.batch()`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models#batch) — many inputs, parallelized

How-to hub: [Chat models | LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/models)

---

## 1. `invoke()`

Pass a string or a list of messages:

```python
message = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.invoke("what is Ramadan? make it super short")
```

Returns an `AIMessage`:

```python
print(message.content)
```

---

## Messages

**Messages** are the unit of context: input and output of the model.

Each message has:

- **Content** — text, images, audio, documents, …
- **Metadata** — usage, ids, …
- **Role** — [`SystemMessage`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/messages#system-message), [`HumanMessage`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/messages#human-message), [`AIMessage`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/messages#ai-message), [`ToolMessage`](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/messages#tool-message)

```python
from langchain.messages import SystemMessage, HumanMessage, AIMessage

system_prompt = SystemMessage("You always answer with 10 concise words, no more.")
user_prompt = HumanMessage("How do I make an Agent in Python?")

messages = [
    system_prompt,
    user_prompt,
]
response = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.invoke(messages)
print(response.content)
```

---

## Metadata: `usage_metadata`

```python
response.usage_metadata
```

---

## Conversation history

```python
conversation = [
    SystemMessage("You are a helpful assistant that translates English to Arabic."),
    HumanMessage("Translate: I love programming."),
    AIMessage("أحب البرمجة."),
    HumanMessage("I love building applications.")
]

message = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.invoke(conversation)
print(message.content)
```

---

## 2. `stream()`

`stream()` yields [`AIMessageChunk`](https://reference.langchain.com/python/langchain-core/messages/ai/AIMessageChunk) objects as they are produced.

```python
chunks = []
for chunk in model_nemotron3_nano_precise.stream("what is Ramadan? keep it short, keep it simple."):
    chunks.append(chunk)
    print(chunk.text, end="", flush=True)
```

Slice some of them:

```python
for ch in chunks[50:60]:
    print(ch.content)
```

More: [Streaming | LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/streaming)

---

## 3. `batch()`

Independent prompts in one call — better throughput / cost characteristics when supported.

```python
responses = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.batch([
    "What is the capital of Saudi Arabia?",
    "What is 2 + 8",
    "Is the sky blue or is it our perception? give a short and concise answer"
])

for response in responses:
    print(response)
```

---

## Structured output

Ask for output matching a schema — easier parsing and downstream logic.

[Pydantic models](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/models/#basic-model-usage) give validation, descriptions, nesting.

```python
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field

class Movie(BaseModel):
    """A movie with details."""
    title: str = Field(..., description="The title of the movie")
    year: int = Field(..., description="The year the movie was released")
    director: str = Field(..., description="The director of the movie")
    rating: float = Field(..., description="The movie's rating out of 10")
```

Configure LLM output with this structure:

```python
model_with_structure = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.with_structured_output(Movie)
response = model_with_structure.invoke(
    "Provide details about the movie Zootropolis"
)

print("Title:", response.title)
print("Year:", response.year)
print("Director:", response.director)
print("Rating:", response.rating)
```

---

## Example: refining a search query

```python
class SearchQuery(BaseModel):
    search_query: str = Field(None, description="Query that is optimized web search.")
    justification: str = Field(
        None, description="Why this query is relevant to the user's request."
    )

structured_llm = model_nemotron3_nano_precise.with_structured_output(SearchQuery)
```

---

## Why structured output?

- **Cost**: the model doesn't generate extra text.
- **Accuracy**: you get only what you want; no more, no less.
- **Programming**: structre can be parsed and used in programs. Example: tool calling.

[Structured output | LangChain](https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/structured-output)

---

## Key takeaways

- Three key methods for models: invoke, stream, and batch.
- LLMs can be configured to responsd in a structured format

**Next step:** open [`01_models.ipynb`](./01_models.ipynb) and run the cells.
